title: Spark Optimisation Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of broadcast joins, partitions, and avoiding OOM errors in Spark.

questions.question: Which Spark join handles a very small dimension table combined with a massive fact table without data skew?
If you notice one Spark task running much longer than the others during a Sort Merge Join, you likely have:

options.text: Data skew caused by a dominant key

questions.question: What is the primary difference between df.repartition() and df.coalesce()?

options.text: Repartition is much faster because it avoids shuffling data over the network
Coalesce decreases the partition count without causing a full shuffle, so it's faster
Coalesce increases the partition count dynamically
Repartition cannot increase partition count past the initial number

questions.question: Why should you avoid using Python UDFs when using the PySpark DataFrame API?

options.text: Because Python doesn't support the required math operations
They cause data to serialize/deserialize between the JVM and Python, breaking Catalyst optimisations
They force Spark to evaluate transformations eagerly

questions.question: What feature introduced in Spark 3 dynamically adjusts query execution plans based on runtime statistics?
